description: Fix 4 failing stress tests in stress-cross-system.e2e.ts. Root causes: (1) permission toggle test — slow_with_progress call fails because side panel permission toggle affects the in-flight call's plugin (not a different tool), (2) config change test — error message regex doesn't match 'Invalid arguments' error, (3) search+install test — timing issue, (4) multi-session test — setup issue.

userStories.title: Fix MCP client + side panel permission toggle test
userStories.description: The test toggles the e2e-test PLUGIN permission (Off→Auto) while a slow_with_progress call from the same plugin is in-flight. The toggle to 'Off' causes the server to reject the in-flight call because plugins.changed fires and the extension re-evaluates readiness. Fix the test to toggle a DIFFERENT plugin's permission (e.g., browser) so the in-flight e2e-test call is truly unaffected.
acceptanceCriteria: Test toggles browser plugin permission (not e2e-test) while e2e-test slow call is in-flight
The in-flight e2e-test slow call completes successfully (isError=false)
Side panel shows the correct permission state for both plugins after
userStories.notes:
Files:
- e2e/stress-cross-system.e2e.ts (fix the permission toggle test)

Context:
The error at line 114:
```
expect(slowValue.isError).not.toBe(true);
```
The slow_with_progress call returns isError=true because toggling the e2e-test plugin to 'Off' triggers plugins.changed which affects the in-flight dispatch. The test's premise was wrong — it claimed to toggle a 'different tool' but actually toggled the same PLUGIN.

Fix: Toggle the BROWSER plugin permission instead. This is truly independent of the e2e-test plugin's dispatch. Change:
- `selectPermission(sp, 'Permission for e2e-test plugin', 'Off')` → `selectPermission(sp, 'Permission for browser tools', 'Off')`
- Then restore: `selectPermission(sp, 'Permission for browser tools', 'Auto')`

Constraints:
- The browser plugin card must be visible in the side panel (may need to scroll or find it)
- The e2e-test slow call MUST be the one verified for success — it should be completely unaffected
- Keep the zero pageerror assertion

userStories.title: Fix CLI config change during dispatch — error regex mismatch
userStories.description: The test expects error text matching /not found|unavailable|removed|disconnected|no matching tab/ but the actual error is 'Invalid arguments for tool'. Fix the regex to include 'Invalid arguments' or fix the test to properly handle the actual error the system produces when a plugin is removed mid-dispatch.
acceptanceCriteria: Error message regex matches the actual system error when plugin is removed mid-dispatch
At least 1 of 3 in-flight calls fails (proving removal took effect)
After plugin restored + POST /reload, fresh echo call succeeds
npx playwright test e2e/stress-cross-system.e2e.ts --grep 'CLI config' passes
userStories.notes:
Files:
- e2e/stress-cross-system.e2e.ts (fix the config-change test)

Context:
The error at line 220:
```
call 0: error should identify plugin removal, got: Invalid arguments for tool "e2e-test_slow_with_progress":
```
The regex `/not found|unavailable|removed|disconnected|no matching tab/i` doesn't match 'Invalid arguments'. This error occurs because after the plugin is removed and rediscovered, the tool schema changes or the tool is no longer recognized.

Fix: Add 'Invalid arguments' and 'unknown tool' to the error regex:
```typescript
/not found|unavailable|removed|disconnected|no matching tab|Invalid arguments|unknown tool|disabled|not been reviewed/i
```

Also consider: the test may need to wait longer for the plugin removal to propagate before checking errors. The 'Invalid arguments' error suggests the tool exists but the plugin state is inconsistent.

Constraints:
- The regex must match ALL possible error messages the system can produce when a plugin is removed mid-dispatch
- Do NOT change the requirement that at least 1 call must fail

userStories.title: Fix side panel search + plugin install test — timing and setup
userStories.description: The search+install test fails because it tries to search while the side panel hasn't fully loaded, or the plugin addition notification doesn't arrive before the assertion. Fix timing and add proper waits.
acceptanceCriteria: Test waits for side panel to fully render before typing in search
After config write + POST /reload, test waits for plugins.changed notification to propagate
E2E Test plugin card appears in search results within 30s timeout
userStories.notes:
Files:
- e2e/stress-cross-system.e2e.ts (fix the search+install test)

Context:
This test starts with no plugins, opens side panel, types 'test' in search, adds plugin via config+reload, and expects the plugin to appear. The failure could be:
1. Side panel not connected when search happens
2. plugins.changed notification not processed before assertion
3. Search debounce timing issue

Fix: Add proper waits:
- Wait for side panel to show 'Connected' state (or browser card visible) before searching
- After POST /reload, wait for server health to show the plugin (plugins >= 1)
- Then give the side panel 2-3s for the plugins.changed notification to propagate
- Use `expect(sp.getByText('E2E Test')).toBeVisible({ timeout: 30_000 })` for the final assertion

Constraints:
- The search input is `sp.locator('input[type="text"]').first()`
- The plugins.changed notification is async — the side panel may need a reload or wait

userStories.title: Fix multiple MCP sessions concurrent test — setup issue
userStories.description: The multi-session test fails because tool calls are made before the extension/tab pipeline is ready. Add proper warmup and ensure all 3 clients can make successful calls before starting the concurrent test.
acceptanceCriteria: All 3 MCP clients wait for extension connection + tab readiness before concurrent calls
A warmup echo call succeeds for each client before the concurrent test begins
15 concurrent calls (5 per client) all succeed with correct prefixed messages
npx playwright test e2e/stress-cross-system.e2e.ts --grep 'Multiple MCP' passes
userStories.notes:
Files:
- e2e/stress-cross-system.e2e.ts (fix the multi-session test)

Context:
The test creates 3 MCP clients and fires 15 concurrent calls. The failure is likely because the extension hasn't connected or the matching tab isn't ready when calls fire.

Fix: After creating all 3 clients and initializing them, add:
```typescript
// Warmup: verify dispatch pipeline is ready for all clients
await waitForToolResult(client1, 'e2e-test_echo', { message: 'warmup-1' }, { isError: false }, 15_000);
await waitForToolResult(client2, 'e2e-test_echo', { message: 'warmup-2' }, { isError: false }, 15_000);
await waitForToolResult(client3, 'e2e-test_echo', { message: 'warmup-3' }, { isError: false }, 15_000);
```

Constraints:
- All 3 clients must warmup BEFORE the concurrent test
- The warmup proves each client's session is established and the dispatch pipeline works
- Keep the session ID uniqueness assertion
